Photo by Cytonn Photography on UnsplashMember-only story

Artificial Intelligence

Quality Assurance

Software Engineering

AI

Leadership

5 min read
Just now

--

A consulting leader’s guide to building a multi-method AI validation program that combines deterministic checks, probabilistic evaluation, human judgment, adversarial pressure, and production monitoring.
AI validation is not a single gate; it is an operating model. Traditional QA asks whether software meets known requirements. AI assurance asks whether a system remains accurate, safe, explainable, secure, and useful across ambiguous inputs, shifting data, changing user behavior, and adversarial conditions.
From pass/fail to confidence
AI testing must produce evidence of reliability over distributions, not merely success on scripted scenarios.
From model evaluation to system validation
The application, prompt, retrieval layer, integration, controls, workflow, and monitoring all need assurance.
From UAT to continuous assurance
Trust must be measured before release and monitored after release as data, users, and risks evolve.
The AI Testing Problem: Trust Is Multi-Dimensional
In conventional enterprise applications, testing is anchored in deterministic logic. If a transaction…
